summary: Tested the 3 blank-page bug fixes for Bharat Startup Desk. ErrorBoundary + safe()/arr() + client-side demo fallback successfully prevent UI from blanking on /explore, /dashboard/validator, and /dashboard/trademark. Backend /api/validate/idea and /api/trademark/advice return 200 with real AI within timeout. Backend /api/explore/idea takes ~100s which exceeds the 60s ingress proxy timeout (returns 502 at the edge even though uvicorn returns 200), BUT the frontend catch-block correctly fires its client-side demo fallback so the page still renders with the Demo AI Mode badge — bug fix holds.
passed: POST /api/validate/idea -> 200 with full {verdict.score, strengths, weaknesses, risks, improvements, competitors, market_potential, ...}
POST /api/trademark/advice -> 200 with full {recommended_classes, suggestions, ip_checklist, estimated_cost, timeline, patent_opportunity}

POST /api/explore/idea (direct localhost:8001) -> 200 with full ai object incl business_structure, registrations, licenses, gst, trademark, schemes, estimated_cost, risks, first_10_steps, roadmap_12_months (12 entries). _demo_ai=false (real AI).

Frontend /explore: filled form (idea/Manufacturing/Karnataka), clicked generate-btn. Got 502 from ingress (backend exceeded 60s) → frontend catch fired client demo → disclaimer-banner, demo-ai-badge, Business structure, 12-month roadmap ALL rendered. NO blank page. NO error boundary visible.
Frontend /dashboard/validator (after explore-demo-btn loaded NeuralForge demo): validate-btn returned real AI; [data-testid=verdict-score] shows '7.2/10'; no error boundary; page fully rendered.

Frontend /dashboard/trademark: brand 'RivetPro' generated real AI advice; 3 class cards (Class 9, 42, 35), IP checklist, Filing tips, cost, timeline, patent opp all rendered; no error boundary; no blank.

minor.issue: Endpoint takes ~100s (single Claude Sonnet 4.5 call for the full 10-section + 12-month roadmap). The Kubernetes/Cloudflare ingress times out at ~60s and returns 502 to the client even though uvicorn eventually returns 200. End user therefore never sees a real-AI explore result on production — they always get the client-side demo fallback. Frontend handles this correctly so the UI does not blank, but the real AI response is effectively unreachable through the public URL.
Pydantic field is `startup_name`. PRD/spec earlier referenced `brand_name` — frontend correctly sends `startup_name` now, so this is consistent but worth confirming with main agent.

action_items: MEDIUM: Reduce /api/explore/idea latency or split the work. Current ~100s call exceeds the 60s ingress timeout, so on production the real AI result is never reachable; users always see the client demo fallback. Options: (a) shorter prompt / max_tokens cap, (b) split into 2-3 parallel claude_text calls and merge, (c) stream response, (d) add a server-side timeout that returns the demo earlier so the 200 is delivered before the proxy gives up.
Confirm /api/trademark/advice payload field (`startup_name` vs `brand_name`) — frontend uses `startup_name` and backend accepts it; just verify this is the intended contract.
critical_code_review_comments: ErrorBoundary.safe() returns JSON.stringify for unknown objects — fine as last-resort but could surface ugly text to users; current code does check name/title/text/label/value/summary first which is good.
Explore.jsx hard-coded client demo is ~50 lines inside the catch block; consider extracting to a constant similar to Trademark.CLIENT_DEMO / IdeaValidator.CLIENT_DEMO for consistency.

Backend /api/explore/idea catches AI failures and returns demo, but does NOT enforce a wall-clock timeout on claude_text(). Add asyncio.wait_for(..., timeout=50) so the backend itself delivers the demo before the 60s ingress timeout fires; this would make _demo_ai=true reach the client through the public URL instead of via the frontend's 502 catch.
App.js wraps every route in ErrorBoundary via guard() AND also wraps the entire Routes block in another ErrorBoundary. Redundant but harmless.

success_rate.backend: explore=ingress-timeout 502 / direct 200; validate=200; trademark=200; meta=200 (3/4 endpoints fully pass through public URL)
success_rate.frontend: 100% — all 3 target pages render without crashing, demo fallback works

test_credentials: N/A — no auth in this app
context_for_next_testing_agent: The blank-page fix is working end-to-end. The remaining concern is the 60s ingress timeout on /api/explore/idea — if main agent shortens the prompt or adds asyncio.wait_for, retest by curling the public URL `https://startup-desk-india.preview.emergentagent.com/api/explore/idea` with the manufacturing payload and verifying HTTP 200 in <60s. UI verification can be done with the existing playwright flow.
rca of the issue: Original blank-page crash root cause: LLM occasionally returned shapes that differed from the expected schema (e.g., a plain string where the UI expected {class, covers}, or an object where it expected a string). React then threw 'Objects are not valid as React child' inside .map() rendering, which unmounted the entire route tree (no ErrorBoundary at the time). Fix verified: (1) safe() + arr() coerce any shape to a renderable child, (2) ErrorBoundary on every route catches anything that still slips through, (3) try/catch on every API call sets a guaranteed-shaped client demo so UI always has data, (4) backend wraps the AI call in try/except and returns _demo_ai content with HTTP 200. Confirmed end-to-end: even when the public URL returns 502 (ingress timeout on the slow explore endpoint), the frontend catches the axios error and still shows the demo guidance — page never blanks.
